tasks.subject: [shapes] [P1] Add `shape` package type + ShapeManifestSchema (plain union member) + top-level union superRefine, and widen DependencyDeclaration
tasks.description: In `packages/marketplace/src/package-types.ts`, append `'shape'` to `PackageTypeSchema` (append-only, preserving order). In `packages/marketplace/src/manifest-schema.ts`, add the browser-safe sub-schemas from spec Â§2 (`ShapeAgentAffinitySchema`, `ShapeAgentSchema`, `ShapeScheduleSchema` including the PermissionMode DRIFT NOTE comment, `ShapeConnectionSchema` discriminated on `kind`, `ShapeLayoutSchema`, `ShapeLineageSchema`) and define `ShapeManifestSchema = BasePackageManifestSchema.extend({ type: literal('shape'), activates, extensions, layout, agents, schedules, connections, lineage })` as a PLAIN ZodObject. Zod 3 constraint (packages/marketplace pins `zod ^3.25.76`): a `z.discriminatedUnion` member must be a plain ZodObject â€” `.superRefine()` returns a ZodEffects and CANNOT be a member (the codebase documents this at `packages/shared/src/schemas.ts` on `OperationProgressEventShapeSchema`), so do NOT attach `.superRefine` to the member. Instead export `shapeCrossFieldChecks(m: ShapePackageManifest, ctx: z.RefinementCtx)` implementing the four rules (Â§2: every `schedules[].agentRef` resolves to an `agents[].ref`; â‰¤1 `default` agent; each `extension-secret` connection's `extension` is in `activates`/`extensions`; each agent has `template` or `matchName`; each violation via `ctx.addIssue` with a precise path) and attach it as a TOP-LEVEL `.superRefine` on `MarketplacePackageManifestSchema` narrowing on `m.type === 'shape'` â€” the union is the install path's parse entry (`packages/marketplace/src/package-validator.ts:165` calls `MarketplacePackageManifestSchema.safeParse`, invoked by installer step 3), so the rules provably fire on every install. TSDoc `ShapeManifestSchema` with the 'validate through MarketplacePackageManifestSchema, never this member alone' warning (the OperationProgressEventShapeSchema pattern). Widen `DependencyDeclarationSchema` regex to allow `shape:`. Export `ShapePackageManifest`. TSDoc every export. Acceptance: `pnpm --filter @dorkos/marketplace typecheck` + lint clean and schema construction does not throw (the ZodEffects-as-member error is the failure this placement avoids); unit tests prove a valid shape manifest parses and round-trips VIA `MarketplacePackageManifestSchema`; each cross-field rule rejects a crafted invalid manifest WHEN PARSED THROUGH THE UNION (not the bare member); `shapeCrossFieldChecks` is exported (task 2.5 reuses it); a drift test asserts `ShapeScheduleSchema`'s permissionMode values equal `PermissionModeSchema`'s six values; `shape:foo@^1.0.0` is accepted while `theme:foo` is still rejected; the four existing package types still parse unchanged.
tasks.activeForm: Adding the shape package type and ShapeManifestSchema

tasks.subject: [shapes] [P1] Add the `apply_layout` variant to UiCommandSchema
tasks.description: In `packages/shared/src/schemas.ts`, add a variant to `UiCommandSchema` (the discriminated union on `action` at ~line 2892): `z.object({ action: z.literal('apply_layout'), shape: z.string().min(1) })`, with a TSDoc note that `shape` is an installed Shape name resolved server-side. Count correction: the union already holds 21 variants while its doc comment (~line 2887) stales at '20' â€” fix the comment to 22 (post-apply_layout), not 21. Acceptance: `pnpm --filter @dorkos/shared typecheck` clean; unit test parses an `apply_layout` command and confirms the union still rejects an unknown `action`; `UiCommand` type infers the new member; the doc comment reads 22 and matches an actual count of the `action: z.literal` members. No dispatcher wiring in this task (that is 3.1).
tasks.activeForm: Adding the apply_layout UI command variant

tasks.subject: [shapes] [P1] Add `ui.shapes` config section (ShapeUserPrefsSchema) with a semver-keyed conf migration
tasks.description: Follow the `adding-config-fields` skill. In `packages/shared/src/config-schema.ts` add `ShapeUserPrefsSchema` (`active: string|null`, `agentDefaults: record<projectPath,string>`, `autoFollowAgent: boolean`) per spec Â§4, add `shapes: ShapeUserPrefsSchema.default(...)` under `UserConfigSchema.ui`, and update the `ui` default object + `USER_CONFIG_DEFAULTS`. Mirror the config PATCH schema in `packages/shared/src/schemas.ts` (the `ui` object around line 2322 that carries `sidebar`). Add the conf migration in `apps/server/src/services/core/config-manager.ts` (semver-keyed, backfilling `ui.shapes` defaults) and regenerate the JSON schema. Acceptance: `pnpm --filter @dorkos/shared typecheck` + `pnpm --filter @dorkos/server typecheck` clean; a migration test asserts an old config without `ui.shapes` gains the defaults; whole-object write semantics documented (deepMerge replaces arrays). No Shape state on `.dork/agent.json`.
tasks.activeForm: Adding the ui.shapes config section and migration

tasks.subject: [shapes] [P1] Author the Linear Ops fixture and the zero-escape-hatch round-trip test
tasks.description: Create `apps/server/src/services/marketplace/fixtures/valid-shape/.dork/manifest.json` containing the exact Linear Ops manifest from spec Â§Worked Example (activates `linear-issues`, `linear-tender` default agent with the `flow__tending-tracker`/`flow__linear-adapter` skills, the `*/15 * * * *` inbox-tick schedule, the `linear_api_key` extension-secret connection, the chrome layout). Add a test in `packages/marketplace` that loads the fixture, validates it through `MarketplacePackageManifestSchema` (the install path's parse entry â€” NOT the bare `ShapeManifestSchema` member, which skips the cross-field rules), asserts it parses with zero errors and round-trips, and asserts the concrete field values (agentRef resolves, single default agent, secret extension is activated) â€” the executable proof of the validation criterion. Acceptance: the fixture validates green through the union; the test fails if any cross-field rule or field mapping regresses.
tasks.activeForm: Authoring the Linear Ops fixture and round-trip test

tasks.subject: [shapes] [P2] Add the install-shape flow, dispatch case, and index.ts wiring (with residue-free failure test)
tasks.description: Following `contributing/marketplace-installs.md` Â§9, create `apps/server/src/services/marketplace/flows/install-shape.ts`: `install(packagePath, manifest, opts)` wraps `runTransaction` with `target = {dorkHome}/shapes/<name>`, stages the package (compiling any inline `extensions` via the existing `ExtensionCompiler`, as `install-plugin.ts` does) but does NOT auto-enable them (activation is `applyShape`'s job â€” Â§6.1), and returns `InstallResult` with `type: 'shape'`. Add the `case 'shape'` to the `dispatchFlow` switch in `marketplace-installer.ts` and add `shapeFlow` to `InstallerDeps`; the switch has NO assertNever/default arm today (its doc comment overclaims compile-time exhaustiveness), so also add an explicit exhaustiveness guard (`default: { const _exhaustive: never = manifest; throw â€¦ }`) so a future sixth type fails loudly. Construct `ShapeInstallFlow` in the `index.ts` marketplace block (gated on `extensionManager && adapterManager`). Acceptance: flow test stages `fixtures/valid-shape` into a temp `dorkHome` and asserts the Shape dir lands and inline extensions are compiled-but-disabled; a forced `activate` throw leaves zero residue at the target (transaction contract, ADR-0304); an integration test via `buildInstallerForTests` installs the fixture end-to-end; the dispatch switch carries the never-typed default arm. `pnpm --filter @dorkos/server typecheck` clean.
tasks.activeForm: Adding the install-shape flow and wiring

tasks.subject: [shapes] [P2] Implement the idempotent apply-shape service with per-piece degradation
tasks.description: Create `apps/server/src/services/shapes/apply-shape.ts` implementing spec Â§5 `applyShape(name, opts) -> { ok, applied, warnings[], offeredAgents[] }`: resolve the installed manifest (missing = the only fatal case); enable `activates` extensions into `UserConfigSchema.extensions.enabled` (missing extension â†’ skip+warn); record connection prompts for unset extension secrets (via `GET /api/extensions/:id/secrets` isSet); create schedules through the tasks service (`CreateTaskRequestSchema`, resolving `agentRef` â†’ agent `projectPath`; missing agent â†’ create disabled+warn; idempotent by name+target); collect suggested/default agents as offers (never auto-create); write the chrome to `ui.*`; honor `ui.shapes.autoFollowAgent` for the arrival agent; record `ui.shapes.active = name`. Inject the extension manager, tasks service, agent registry, and config manager so it is unit-testable. Acceptance: unit tests (fake collaborators) cover (a) all-present â†’ every extension enabled, every schedule enabled, default agent offered, `active` recorded; (b) EACH degradation row in spec Â§7 emits exactly its warning with `ok: true`; (c) applying twice creates no duplicate schedules and identical config (idempotency). `pnpm --filter @dorkos/server typecheck` + lint clean.

tasks.subject: [shapes] [P2] Implement shape fork with lineage metadata and capture-current
tasks.description: Create `apps/server/src/services/shapes/fork.ts`: clone `{dorkHome}/shapes/<name>` â†’ `<newName>` through `runTransaction`, rewrite the new manifest's `name`, and stamp `lineage = { forkedFrom: '<name>@<source>', forkedFromVersion, forkedAt }` per spec Â§6.2. Support `captureCurrent`: when forking the active Shape, snapshot the live chrome (`ui.*`), the currently-enabled extension ids among the Shape's `activates` candidates, and Shape-originated schedules into the new manifest (resolve Open Question Q2: capture only Shape-originated schedules). Acceptance: unit tests assert the clone stamps lineage and rewrites `name` while the ORIGINAL manifest stays byte-identical; `captureCurrent` snapshots live chrome; a fork of a missing Shape errors cleanly with zero residue. `pnpm --filter @dorkos/server typecheck` clean.

tasks.subject: [shapes] [P2] Add routes/shapes.ts (list/apply/fork), OpenAPI registration, route tests, and the CLI fork command
tasks.description: Create `apps/server/src/routes/shapes.ts` with `GET /api/shapes` (installed Shapes: name, displayName, active flag, lineage), `POST /api/shapes/:name/apply` (drives `apply-shape.ts` and returns its exact Â§5 value `{ ok, applied, warnings[], offeredAgents[] }` â€” `applied` carries `{ layout, activatedExtensions, schedulesCreated }` so the client applies chrome from the response without a second fetch), and `POST /api/shapes/:name/fork` (body `{ as?, captureCurrent? }`). Register the routes + Zod response schemas in the OpenAPI registry (keep `openapi-fresh` green â€” regenerate the snapshot). Mount the router in `index.ts` under the marketplace block. Add a `dorkos shape fork <name> [--as <newName>]` CLI subcommand (thin HTTP client, matching the existing install/uninstall command shape in `packages/cli/src/commands/`). Acceptance: route tests (supertest-style, injected fake services) assert each endpoint's contract incl. `applied` and the degradation `warnings[]` passthrough; `pnpm --filter @dorkos/server typecheck` + the openapi snapshot check pass; CLI command hits the route.
tasks.activeForm: Adding shape routes and the CLI fork command

tasks.subject: [shapes] [P2] Add shape-aware conflict detection, validator, and the `scaffold shape` generator
tasks.description: Extend `apps/server/src/services/marketplace/conflict-detector.ts` with shape-aware checks (warn when an installed Shape already owns the target name; inline-extension id collisions) per spec Â§6.1. Extend the `@dorkos/marketplace` validator so `dorkos package validate` accepts and checks a `type: 'shape'` package (reuses the exported `shapeCrossFieldChecks` from task 1.1 â€” the same rules the union runs â€” and reports dangling `agentRef`/secret/agent issues as human-readable errors). Add a `dorkos package scaffold shape` generator that emits a minimal valid Shape manifest skeleton (name, empty layout, one suggested agent template, commented schedule/connection stubs). Acceptance: validator tests reject the four crafted-invalid manifests with clear messages; scaffolder output validates green; conflict-detector test covers the name-collision warning. `pnpm --filter @dorkos/marketplace typecheck` + lint clean.
tasks.activeForm: Adding conflict detection, validator, and scaffolder support

tasks.subject: [shapes] [P3] Wire the apply_layout dispatcher case and applyShape context
tasks.description: In `apps/client/src/layers/shared/lib/ui-action-dispatcher.ts`, add `case 'apply_layout': ctx.applyShape?.(command.shape); break;` mirroring the existing `switch_agent` optional-context pattern (line 275), and add `applyShape?: (shape: string) => void` to the dispatcher context type. Provide the real `applyShape` implementation from the app shell: call `POST /api/shapes/:name/apply` and apply the response's `applied.layout` (the resolved chrome carried in the Â§5/Â§9 contract â€” no second fetch) via the existing panel/sidebar dispatch + the W1c live extension re-mount. Acceptance: `ui-action-dispatcher.test.ts` gains a case asserting `apply_layout` calls `ctx.applyShape` with the name and that an absent context is a safe no-op (matching `switch_agent`); `pnpm --filter @dorkos/client typecheck` clean. Note: depends on W1a (`switchAgent` wired) for the arrival-agent follow-through.

tasks.subject: [shapes] [P3] Build the features/shapes slice â€” switcher UI, offers surface, and degradation warnings
tasks.description: Create a `features/shapes` FSD slice in `apps/client/src/layers/features/shapes/` (respecting the layer rule; import via barrels): a Shape switcher (lists `GET /api/shapes`, applies via the dispatcher, shows the active Shape), a 'Reset to Shape defaults' action (re-apply), an offers surface (arrival agent accept â†’ agent-create; connection prompts â†’ the existing extension-secret PUT flow), and a degradation warnings toast fed by the apply response `warnings[]`. Use TanStack Query for server state and the mock `Transport`/`TransportProvider` in tests. Depends on W1a (`switchAgent`) and W1c (live extension re-mount) for a reload-free switch. Acceptance: RTL tests cover applyâ†’active, reset, an offered agent accept, and a degradation warning render; mobile/tablet/desktop layouts verified; `pnpm --filter @dorkos/client typecheck` + lint clean.
tasks.activeForm: Building the shapes switcher and offers UI

tasks.subject: [shapes] [P4] Write contributing/shapes.md and patch marketplace-installs.md
tasks.description: Following the `writing-developer-guides` skill, author `contributing/shapes.md` (authoring a Shape manifest with the Linear Ops worked example; the install-vs-apply split; the degradation model; fork/lineage; the affinity-not-ownership rule and where reverse affinity lives). Add it to `contributing/INDEX.md`. Patch `contributing/marketplace-installs.md` to add the Shape flow to the flows list and document that install stages while apply activates (Â§6.1). Acceptance: `/docs:coverage` passes (guide indexed); prose follows the developer-guide structure; no unverified claims (the switching UX is described as designed, not as shipped, until P3 lands).

tasks.subject: [shapes] [P4] Extract ADRs from the spec via /adr:from-spec
tasks.description: Run `/adr:from-spec` on `specs/shapes/` and, applying the significance rubric, record the load-bearing decisions as ADRs: composition-manifest-not-monolith; reverse-affinity-and-active-shape-state-in-user-config (extends ADR 260717-001409); shape-layout-excludes-the-agent-list-filing; switch-offers-not-forces-the-default-agent; preset-semantics-with-fork-to-capture (no write-back). Cross-link ADR-0230 (fifth package type) and ADR-0304 (install transaction). Acceptance: each ADR passes `/adr:review` quality; `decisions/manifest.json` updated; the spec's Related ADRs section back-links the new ids.
